rationale: Y explicitly surfaces the two concerns that mattered most in the real review — the auto-detected-vs-post-override pipeline restriction bug (confirming it's now correctly resolved via get_pipeline_config, matching hsliuustc0106's concern and the added regression test) and the trust_remote_code default flip, correctly reasoning it as a security-positive change worth confirming (matching the PR author's own stated rationale). X flags the same trust_remote_code change as its headline 'major' but argues backwards — suggesting reverting to a True default, which is the opposite of both vLLM's own convention and the author's stated intent — weakening its precision on its top finding. X's second major (Qwen2.5-Omni parity) is plausible but unverifiable from the given diff and not echoed in ground truth. Both are well-grounded with file:line citations and concrete asks; X is terser and slightly more directive, but Y's broader, more correctly-reasoned coverage of confirmed reviewer concerns gives it better recall and precision overall.
